_provenance.source: FICTIONAL demo names — invented for the public demo; NOT real businesses. (The real-data CAPABILITY is intact: lib/ingest/sf-adapter.ts ingests real DataSF 'Registered Business Locations'.)
_provenance.dataset_id: g8m3-pdis (the real adapter source; this displayed fixture is fictional)
_provenance.endpoint: https://data.sfgov.org/resource/g8m3-pdis.json (real adapter source; not used to build this fictional fixture)
_provenance.license: PDDL 1.0 (the real DataSF adapter source is public-domain; the fictional names in THIS fixture carry no license constraint)
_provenance.selection: PUBLIC DEMO uses 20 obviously-FICTIONAL names (10 Restaurant + 10 Retail) so the SYNTHETIC 'high-risk / stalled' activation states are never attached to real businesses or people (a privacy/optics risk on a public surface). The real-data path — real DataSF names, sanitized, PII-scrubbed, NAICS-crosswalked (Food Services->Restaurant, Retail Trade->Retail) — is implemented + tested in lib/ingest/sf-adapter.ts + evals/hybrid-dataset.test.ts; swap this fixture for a real export via the adapter's documented contract.
_provenance.synthetic_note: Names are fictional; the activation state (onboarding progress, blockers, risk inputs) is SYNTHETIC, applied deterministically at load by lib/ingest/overlay.ts — no public dataset exists for it.
